title: MailerLite vs Moosend (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare MailerLite and Moosend email marketing tools. See pricing, features, ratings, and which is best for your business.
h1: MailerLite vs Moosend: Detailed Comparison for 2025
intro: Both MailerLite and Moosend are popular freemium email marketing platforms offering similar starting prices and free tier subscriber limits. This comparison helps you understand the key differences in features, pricing, and customer satisfaction to determine which tool best fits your email marketing needs.
choose_a_if: You need e-commerce integrations for an online store and want a platform built with sellers in mind
You prefer a user-friendly interface designed for beginners with excellent drag-and-drop functionality
You're looking for pop-ups and survey features to boost engagement alongside email marketing
choose_b_if: You want unlimited emails sent to your contacts without worrying about sending limits
Built-in A/B testing is important for optimizing your email campaigns and improving performance
You need real-time analytics to track campaign results and subscriber behavior as it happens

faq.a: Neither is universally better—it depends on your needs. Moosend edges ahead overall with unlimited emails and A/B testing, earning a slightly higher G2 rating (4.7 vs 4.6). However, MailerLite excels for e-commerce businesses with native integrations and offers superior beginner-friendly design. Choose based on your specific requirements rather than general superiority.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, MailerLite or Moosend?
faq.a: Both platforms are equally priced, starting at $0 for free tiers with 1,000 subscribers and $9/month for paid plans. The real cost difference emerges if you send high email volumes—Moosend's unlimited emails feature could be more economical at scale, while MailerLite's pricing remains tied to sending limits.

faq.q: Can I switch from Moosend to MailerLite?
faq.a: Yes, you can migrate your subscriber list and campaign data between platforms. Most email marketing tools support CSV exports and imports, making the transition relatively straightforward. However, you may lose some historical data or formatting during the migration, so plan accordingly and test thoroughly before switching completely.
